title: dactylis hispanica subsp. lusitanica
intro: Dactylis hispanica subsp. lusitanica, also known as Lusitanian orchard grass, is a subspecies of the Dactylis hispanica plant, a perennial grass native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Dactylis hispanica subsp. lusitanica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
